excerpt: The Prophet resolved to undertake his first and final pilgrimage in the tenth year of the Hijra, thus proclaiming the Farewell Pilgrimage.
source_location_hint: Sermons and teachings on the Prophet's final pilgrimage

excerpt: In this verse, Allah made known a grave warning: should the Prophet fail to convey what he was commanded to convey, all his labors in Prophethood would be rendered void.
source_location_hint: Commentary on verse 5:67 and the event of Ghadir Khum

excerpt: The Prophet's wisdom lay thus: he desired that this stern verse be revealed so that the supreme importance of Ali's wilayat would be established beyond all doubt.
source_location_hint: Exegesis of the Prophet's hesitation and the revelation of Quran 5:67

excerpt: For thirty years, these successive rulers inflicted upon Ali and the Ahl al-Bayt a cascade of indignities and wrongs, yet Ali and his companions endured all such trials in silence.
source_location_hint: Discourses on the trials of the Ahl al-Bayt during the early caliphates

excerpt: For thirty years he refrained from asserting his rights, knowing that had he done so, the Umma would have splintered into countless fragments, like a child torn between two mothers.
source_location_hint: Parable of the two mothers and the child; Ali's forbearance

excerpt: Whoever possesses the true knowledge of these five attains the highest stations of faith. They know all that Allah the Mighty has created; they are the Word of Piety and Treasuries of the heavens and earth.
source_location_hint: Discourse of Imam Ja'far on the Panjtan (Five Pure Ones)

excerpt: Allah created Muhammad and Ali from one Divine Light—a radiance emanating from the grandeur and majesty of the Godhead. No other being was formed from this light save these two.
source_location_hint: Cosmology and the creation of the luminous essences of the Prophets and Imams

excerpt: Had it not been for these noble ones, no knowledge of Allah would exist; there would be no understanding of how to worship the Most Merciful in truth.
source_location_hint: Necessity of the Ahl al-Bayt for the guidance of creation

excerpt: When night falls and you have completed the day, sit alone and address your soul, remembering all your deeds and seeking forgiveness, accepting the wilayat of Amir al-Mu'minin.
source_location_hint: Sermon on spiritual self-reckoning (muhasaba al-nafs)

excerpt: When Zulaikha came to know the Messenger Muhammad through love born from hearing his description, the Lord commanded Yusuf to marry her, saying: 'I love her for the love of My Beloved born in her heart.'
source_location_hint: Esoteric interpretation of the story of Yusuf and Zulaikha; love for the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: On the night of the Miʿraj, the Prophet conversed with the angels, who revealed that all creation learns from Muhammad and Ali, and that every descent and ascent of deeds passes through them.
source_location_hint: Session on the station of the Prophet and Ali, the angels' greeting

excerpt: A slave girl in Madinah, blind and dark-faced, testified to the wilayat of Ali. When asked by a follower (Khamdar), her sight was restored through prayer blessing her faith.
source_location_hint: Miraculous accounts of the spiritual power of wilayat

excerpt: Through the wilayat of Ali, sincerity in the worship of Allah is perfected. To acknowledge the Imams is to give zakat; this is the established religion of Allah.
source_location_hint: Esoteric interpretation of the pillars of Islam in relation to wilayat

excerpt: Sending blessings on the Prophet alone will not benefit you unless you accept the wilayat of Ali. Allah united us in one reality yet distinguished us by attributes.
source_location_hint: Unity of Muhammad and Ali in the spiritual realm; twin witnesses

excerpt: My name is inscribed upon the Throne through which it was established, written in the heavens through which they were raised up, upon the earth, mountains, air, and all creation.
source_authority: Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman (early Ismaili da'i)
source_location_hint: Cosmological role of the Imam; his name inscribed in creation

excerpt: O Salman, you have found my attributes recorded in the Torah and Gospel in the very same manner. You are the proof of Allah through whom He accepted Adam's repentance and saved Joseph.
source_location_hint: The testimony of Salman al-Farsi to the exalted station of Ali

excerpt: On the Day of Resurrection, the pulpits of light will be established for all the prophets, and the Prophet's will be the highest; then Ali's; then the Imams Hasan and Husayn
source_location_hint: early Islamic tradition narrated from Imam Jaʿfar al-Sadiq

excerpt: Fatima is honored in the assembly; angels bear her throne; she looks back to recognize her station; she selects her lovers and brings them to Paradise
source_location_hint: eschatological tradition from Imam Jaʿfar al-Sadiq

excerpt: On that Day, none remain in Hell except the disbelievers and hypocrites; those who denied Fatima's wilayat speak in regret
source_location_hint: narrated from Imam Jaʿfar al-Sadiq

excerpt: Fatima: 'I drew so near to God that but two bowlengths separated her from Him,' alluding to Qur'an 53:9 on the Prophet's proximity
source_location_hint: virtues of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: During the Hijra, the Prophet entrusted Ali with returning the trusts of the disbelievers; no one later complained that Ali failed in this duty, showing mutual understanding between them
source_location_hint: events of the Hijra

excerpt: He who dies loving Ali, Hasan, Husayn, and the Imams meets them in a state of peace, gladness, and brightness; when the soul reaches the throat, angels welcome him
source_authority: Imam al-Baqir via Ali ibn al-Husayn
source_location_hint: spiritual rewards for love of the Imams

excerpt: You are with whom you love, and you shall receive what your deeds have earned
source_location_hint: on the reward of love and deeds

excerpt: We are the rope of God, the guides to guidance, standards and signs for the peoples. Whosoever grasped us was saved; whosoever abandoned us drowned in ignorance. We are mercy to creation and ward off punishment from you.
source_location_hint: on the role and station of the Imams

excerpt: Six forms accompany the believer in the grave: Prayer, Zakat, Fasting, Hajj, righteous deeds, and most beautiful is the wilayat of the Family of Muhammad
source_author: From Imam al-Baqir or Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq
source_location_hint: on the six forms in the grave

excerpt: There is no kinship between us and God save through obedience. Whosoever obeys God and loves us is our friend; whosoever disobeys God, our love avails him not.
source_author: From Imam al-Baqir to Jabir al-Ansari
source_location_hint: instruction on wilayat and obedience

excerpt: Adam was created by God's hand and received His Spirit only through the wilayat of Ali; God spoke to Moses and established Jesus as a sign only through Ali; no creature is worthy of God's sight save through servitude to us (the Ahl al-Bayt)
source_location_hint: on the necessity of wilayat in divine creation and prophecy

excerpt: We are Prayer, Zakat, Fasting, Hajj, the Sacred City, the Kaaba, the Qibla, the Face of God; wherever you turn is the Face of God; we are God's signs and proofs.
source_location_hint: on the cosmic and spiritual role of the Ahl al-Bayt

excerpt: The Prophet's moral character during his first forty years exceeded the combined moral achievement of all 124,000 prophets; this is why in Duʿaʾ Hayat al-Qaf, his moral excellence is specially invoked alongside the miracles of other prophets.
source_work: Tradition cited from Tuhfat al-Sair fi Sharh al-Muʿjam al-Nawadir
source_location_hint: on the Prophet's pre-prophetic moral example

excerpt: The Qur'an addresses the prophets by their names—O Moses, O Jesus, O Abraham—but the Prophet Muhammad is never called by name alone; rather, he is invoked by titles of honor and affection: Taha, Yasin, al-Muzammil, al-Muddaththir.
source_location_hint: modes of divine address to the prophets

excerpt: The Prophet said to Ali: 'O Ali, if it were not for us, God would not have created Adam, Eve, Paradise, the heavens, or the earth'—indicating the supreme metaphysical station of the Prophet and Ali.
source_location_hint: on the pre-existence and cosmic role of the Prophet and the Imams
